  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Evacuation decay test failed",
    "Pressure rose from 464 microns to 1824 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Significant rise above 500 microns indicates a leak or excessive moisture in the system"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate the micron gauge and pressure test the system with dry nitrogen to locate leaks",
    "Repair any identified leaks and re-evacuate the system",
    "Perform another standing decay test to ensure pressure holds below 500 microns"
  ],
  "rationale": "The system was evacuated to 464 microns, but upon isolation, the pressure rose to 1824 microns over 10 minutes. A standard decay test requires the system to hold below 500 microns (or at least not rise above 1000 microns depending on the specific standard, though rising past 1800 microns clearly indicates a leak or significant moisture/outgassing). This rapid rise represents a failed evacuation test.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation Procedures (holding under 500 microns)"
  ]
}
